papers.title: SimWorlds: A Multi-Agent System for Dynamic 3D Scene Creation
papers.abstract_snippet: LLM agents are increasingly used to translate natural language into 3D scenes in a procedural way, but existing systems focus on static output. Dynamic 4D scenes from text alone, in which liquids flow, particles emit, rigid bodies cascade, and articulated mechanisms move, remain largely unexplored despite their value as editable content and as physics-grounded training data for video generation and embodied AI. Two challenges set the dynamic case apart from static text-to-scene work: an agent mu
papers.reason: 纯3D场景生成与LLM代理研究，无机器人操作或Embodied AI实验，属于计算机视觉/图形学范畴。

papers.title: Neuro-Symbolic Safety Guidance for Vision-Language-Action Models via Constrained Flow Matching
papers.authors: William English, Hao Zheng, Rickard Ewetz
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.01378v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have demonstrated promising generalization capabilities across robotic manipulation tasks, yet their real-world deployment remains limited by the lack of effective safety measures. Specifically, existing safety measures only prevent collisions caused by the robot's next action. papers.title: Imagining the Sense of Touch: Touch-Informed Manipulation via Imagined Tactile Representations

papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.01684v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tactile sensing can substantially improve contact-rich robotic manipulation, yet its practical deployment remains limited by the fragility, calibration requirements, and maintenance burden of tactile hardware. This raises a fundamental question: can robots benefit from tactile knowledge without requiring tactile sensors at deployment? papers.authors: Hexian Ni, Tao Lu, Yinghao Cai
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.01721v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reward design remains a central challenge in reinforcement learning (RL). Hand-crafted rewards are often difficult to specify and may lead to suboptimal policies, while learned rewards from preferences can suffer from inefficiency and unstable training. papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.01804v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) foundation models have recently achieved strong progress in embodied intelligence. To reduce policy-call frequency while preserving temporal coherence, most generative policies adopt an action chunk mechanism, executing multiple future actions in an open-loop manner under a fixed action horizon. papers.reason: 提出轻量级检测-校正推理模块，动态调整动作执行 horizon，可即插即用提升现有VLA模型的时序一致性和效率。

Existing visual-language-action models and world models struggle with accurate 3D geometry and physically meaningful forecasting. papers.title: Guided Action Flow: Q-Guided Inference for Flow-Matching Vision-Language-Action Policies
papers.authors: Liuhaichen Yang, Zhuang Jiang, Chenchao Sheng, Zezhi Tang
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.02092v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Flow-matching vision-language-action policies generate robot action chunks through an iterative transport process, creating an opportunity for test-time guidance without retraining the base policy. papers.title: Actuator Reality Shaping for Zero-Shot Sim-to-Real Robot Learning

papers.title: The Moving Eye: Enhancing VLA Spatial Generalization via Hybrid Dynamic Data Collection
papers.authors: Jincheng Tang, Yilong Zhu, Zhengyuan Xie, Jiang-Jiang Liu, Jiaxing Zhang
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.02322v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have shown remarkable promise in generalized robotic manipulation. Models often fall into the trap of Shortcut Learning, latching onto spurious correlations (e.g., fixed relative poses between objects or between the camera and robot base) rather than learning true spatial relationships.
papers.reason: 提出混合动态数据收集策略以改善VLA的空间泛化能力，通过主动视角选择解决静态视角不足的问题，具有数据层面的实用价值。

papers.title: Learning to Move Before Learning to Do: Task-Agnostic pretraining for VLAs
papers.authors: Junhao Shi, Siyin Wang, Xiaopeng Yu, Li Ji, Jingjing Gong, Xipeng Qiu
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2607.02466v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are fundamentally bottlenecked by the scarcity of expert demonstrations -- triplets of observations, instructions, and actions that are costly to collect at scale.
